name: trove-cycle
description: Every 15 min: query agent verdict + Aave/Merkl rewards. If movable: execute rebalance. If rewards exist: claim via Merkl (no setClaimer required) or Aave (with setClaimer). Log everything to 0G Storage and commit memoryHash + decision counter to iNFT on Galileo.

config._note: tick endpoint pre-builds calldata when source+target are both supported (Aave→Aave currently). Status template if fields are null.
Harvest = claim+swap+redeposit. Adapter status template until protocol-specific calldata is wired.

Server-prebuilt claim tx. Auto-prefers Merkl (where most Aave Base rewards flow). Merkl proofs hardcode recipient — no setClaimer required for Merkl. Aave native claim path via separate workflow if needed (requires user setClaimer).
Increments on-chain decision counters. /api/agent/tick now returns iNftCommit.decisionKind (0=hold, 1=move, 2=harvest) so we don't need ternary expressions in this config.

_design_notes: Two parallel HTTP nodes (tick + rewards_check) fire from cron — checks run in parallel, max latency = slowest endpoint.
Switch expressions use simple equality on string/numeric values — no ternaries, no JS-style operators (defensive: works on most workflow DSLs without modification).
All decision-kind logic moved server-side: /api/agent/tick now returns iNftCommit.decisionKind directly (0=hold, 1=move, 2=harvest). No expression evaluation in the workflow.
branch_should_record routes HOLD verdicts directly to commit_inft, skipping the recordDecision counter increment. MOVE/HARVEST go through both — counter then memory.
wait_mode: any_complete on convergence nodes (upload_memory, commit_inft) means workflow proceeds when ANY upstream node completes — handles the parallel branch pattern correctly.

Galileo (chainId 16602) tx nodes assume KeeperHub Turnkey supports the network. If unsupported, run only the Base subset (rebalance/harvest/claim_rewards/upload_memory) and commit iNFT updates from a separate process using the agent's runtime PRIVATE_KEY.
_activation_gates: Set USER_ADDRESS in env_vars to the user's wallet (one workflow per user).
Fund KeeperHub Turnkey wallet (0x1A09…fdeDDd) with Base ETH for Aave/Merkl claim gas (~$0.30/claim, $5 buys ~15 cycles).
For Aave native claim: user calls Aave RewardsController.setClaimer(user, KEEPERHUB_TURNKEY_WALLET) — UI button at https://trove.web3wagmi.com/.
For Galileo iNFT updates: confirm KeeperHub Turnkey supports chain 16602 OR fall back to runtime PRIVATE_KEY signer.
